source_title: A Model for the 21st Century Newsroom
author: Paul Bradshaw

extracts.extract: The strengths of the online medium are essentially twofold, and contradictory: speed, and depth.
extracts.chapter: Building on the strengths of the medium
extracts.relevance_rationale: The tension between speed and depth in online journalism remains a defining editorial challenge

extracts.extract: Customisation: the final stage should be automatic: the ability for users to customise information to their own needs. This means production processes that integrate things like metatagging, and interfaces that can run off a database, and last but not least, a culture that thinks in terms of these possibilities.
extracts.chapter: A model for the 21st century newsroom: pt1 – the news diamond
extracts.relevance_rationale: LLM-powered personalization and semantic tagging now go far beyond manual metatagging and database-driven interfaces

extracts.extract: Just as the inverted pyramid was partly a result of the increasing role of the telegraph in the news industry, and dominant cultural ideas of empiricism and science, this news diamond attempts to illustrate the change from a 19th century product (the article) to a 21st century process: the iterative journalism of new media; the story that is forever 'unfinished'.
extracts.relevance_rationale: The news diamond concept of iterative, never-finished journalism is even more relevant in the LLM era of continuous updating
